input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Behaviors>
        <toolkit:EventToCommandBehavior
            EventName="Refreshing"
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}" />
    </CollectionView.Behaviors>

    <!-- Pull-to-refresh wrapper -->
    <RefreshView
        Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
        IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

        <CollectionView
            ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
            SelectionMode="Single"
            SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
            SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">

            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                    <Grid Padding="10">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    </Grid>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>
    </RefreshView>
</CollectionView>
```

> **Note:** `CollectionView` doesn't natively support pull-to-refresh — wrap it in a `RefreshView`. The simplified version:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IMyApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelectedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null; // clear selection on return
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false; // must set false to stop spinner
        }
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | Drives the list from your `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | Allows exactly one item selected at a time |
| `SelectedItem → SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | Syncs selection state; set to `null` to deselect |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires when selection changes — triggers navigation |
| `RefreshView.Command → RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull-to-refresh gesture |
| `IsRefreshing` (TwoWay) | Set to `false` when done loading to hide the spinner |

**Tips:**
- Always set `IsRefreshing = false` in a `finally` block to avoid a stuck spinner.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigation so the same item can be re-selected.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) for boilerplate-free bindings.
